reviews.name: Quality Air Conditioning Company
reviews.review_text:
I appreciate their honest feedback of my unit.  One supplier wanted to replace parts This company advises against it as it would not impact the performance of my unit.

Update: Despite them advising me to not make any changes to my AC unit and me agreeing they took it upon themselves make repairs later that week while I was at work.  Based on this I have to take my 5 star down to the lowest rating. We reached an agreement on how much I would pay them for the unwanted work. I will work with another contractor to figure out why my AC is no longer working and to replace it later this year.  My apologies for the delayed update. Been a long 2 months.

Follow-up: My apologies for the “surprise”,  I know how horrible they can be, first hand!

A colleague  wanted to know if I would recommend you all as we are both dealing with HVAC issues over the past week. I briefed them on the experience which consists of me being  “surprised” with repairs and a bill 2 days after a mutual agreement between your company(your tech and the person they called in your office) and myself to not make any repairs outlined in the written agreement from the week prior. This discussion reminded me that I never updated my review.

Was the “surprise” resolved? Yes. After a few discussions about what happened I paid over half of the bill for my “surprise” repairs.  I figured I have new hoses I will need the hoses when I get my new unit later this spring. I didn’t want you all calling me about the bill any longer so I put it to bed on the last call from you all. Despite the fact that I shouldn’t have to go through this as you all touched my belongings without consistent.  However, it was put in writing, no more surprises!

Was it a pleasant overall experience, from my perspective? No

Would I recommend your company based on my experience? No

Once again, my apologies for the “surprise” of me updating this review based on my expertise with your company. However, the initial feedback from your company was refreshing positive experience and I wanted to spread the word as soon as your tech left. It was not an accurate reflection of the experience as it unfolded over the next month, as my  “surprise” repair and bill came after what I thought was the completion of a pleasant interaction with your company.

I do a lot of business based on recommendations and reviews. Therefore, I wanted to be as transparent as I could be from my perspective for others like me.

As I told you earlier over the phone I don’t want anything just catching up on my todo list.  Best wishes.

Final update: I hired another company to revisit the repairs completed by this company. The had to redo one repair and complete the other repare. I had to pay twice over for the repairs, however, the other company has my unit up and running!
reviews.owner_answer:
Hi Chris,
After looking into this, it was brought to my attention that you approved the repairs in writing. You then changed your mind and told us verbally the repairs were not approved. Somehow, there was a miscommunication and we proceeded with the repairs based on your written approval. We also provided you a very large discount on the repair due to the miscommunication. We would have preferred to hash this out two months ago and make things right with you. We thought you were satisfied with the solution as we never heard back from you. It was a surprise to see you leave this review over two months later.

This is now my second excellent experience with Quality Air in the last month. I have a unit that I recently completely "Gut" renovated. Everything was replaced. The HVAC unit has been problematic since installation 18 months ago. The unit went down over Labor Day weekend. I called for an appointment and requested Steve who impressed me very much with his knowledge on my first call.
The unit required a pumping mechanism for the condensation outflow. The existing piping was above the HVAC unit thereby going against gravity. This is why the unit was repeatedly having a clogged drain. It was installed improperly given the space restraints and the plumbing set up. Steve also installed an internal Blue Light system to prevent algae formation. His work was exceptional and again, I would highly recommend Quality Air.

reviews.name: Quality Air Conditioning Company
reviews.review_text: In March a new Bosch compressor was installed in my unit.  I have been without AC for weeks and its 82 degrees in my unit..  Techs have been out numerous times always saying it something different.  The original diagnosis was the freeze sensor that toggles the ac off so it doesn't freeze up.  I could flick the breaker to get it running for another 12-24 hours. But at least I had AC.  The tech messed with it on Friday and now it wont switch on and they wont put it back so I can bypass the sensor.   I was told in early May that they were going to the office to get a new sensor and they would install the next day.  Then  for some reason the decision was made not to install the sensor, but instead diagnose a multitude of causes all that don't work, wiring from the thermostat, wiring from the building, sensor, panel.  They keep pouring freon in to it daily.  In June the tech arrived again and worked on it almost daily for a week.  They arrive Friday evening and left again with no ac and no way to bypass the sensor. The tech said the office should call in a few minutes. No call all weekend and no one answers back from the on call requests.  Got a call Monday morning they would be out between 12 -3pm to fix the problem finally. At 3:10 PM. The technician arrived and said it is not the problem. They were thinking after all this has been misdiagnosed about eight times so another night without AC.  Tuesday tech showed up not in the morning as promised but in the afternoon.  Guess what I still have no AC and its a new issue, water pressure to my unit and there is nothing they can do even though no one in my building is having any issues with water pressure.

Jennifer,
We have done everything possible to help you. There were manufacturer defects on the units and unfortunately that is out of our control. We have been communicating with Bosch and doing our best to have this resolved as fast as we can.

Quality then called the following Monday quoting $7000.00 for a new AC.     I then found out same thing happened to another person in our building.
reviews.owner_answer:
Lynn,
This was a misdiagnosis of your system. It is not common, but does happen from time to time.  
We take full responsibility for the misdiagnosis and acknowledge that we didn't meet our usual standards of service. Our technician made an honest mistake, and we should have been more thorough in our diagnosis.

We're glad to hear that another company was able to resolve the issue by cleaning the drain. We will use this as an opportunity to review our procedures and provide additional training to our technicians to prevent similar mistakes in the future.

We value your feedback and would like to make things right. We'd like to offer you a full refund of the $99 diagnostic fee and a gesture of goodwill for the inconvenience caused.

If you would be willing, we'd appreciate the opportunity to discuss this further and ensure that our services meet your expectations in the future.

Please contact us directly so we can make amends.

Sincerely,
Dave Sasson
GM/VP

reviews.name: Quality Air Conditioning Company
reviews.review_text:
Our AC was installed in 2006 by a small company. When the owner retired in 2018, he moved his client list to another provider. I used them for a few minor repairs, as well a yearly maintenance call ONE WEEK ago. When my AC fan stopped working, I called to get them to come out and fix the problem.
Much to my surprise, they were unresponsive. and the office manager was downright hostile. I was stunned and while I won't name them in this review, you can BET I will be very blunt in my separate review of THEM.
So, I took a chance and called Quality, which seemed to have good reviews and no pending litigation -that I could find by snooping around. The customer service rep, Kelly, was very sympathetic and professional. I was assured that I would be contacted by a technician between 2-6 PM, same day. I was hopeful that was true.
At about 2:30, the technician-Andras-called and said he was on his way. He arrived at 3:05 and immediately diagnosed the problem. Despite having to wait out a few rain delays, he worked on the unit and got it up and running. He also recommended another repair but did not have the parts with him. We agreed to have him return the nest day. But let me emphasize--he FIXED the problem, but offered us another repair that really seemed reasonably priced and would help prolong the life of our unit.
When you have a 2006 AC, you steel yourself for the worst. It was reassuring when the company-specifically Andras-put my fears to rest and simply fixed the problem. There was no hard sell for the second repair. I made the decision because we have taken excellent card of our AC and faithfully had it serviced every year or more since 2008, when we bought the house.
I whole heartedly recommend Quality. Every team member I interacted with was pleasant, calm and professional. When I have a problem in the future (and I WILL. It's 15 years old!) I will call Quality and ask for Andras. Don't bother with the competitors and their silly songs and sappy commercials. Call Quality!
